verses.latin_vulgate: Intellectus Asaph.   Attendite, popule meus, legem meam ;  inclinate aurem vestram in verba oris mei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַשְׂכִּ֗יל לְאָ֫סָ֥ף הַאֲזִ֣ינָה עַ֭מִּי תּוֹרָתִ֑י הַטּ֥וּ אׇ֝זְנְכֶ֗ם לְאִמְרֵי־פִֽי׃
verses.king_james: Give ear, O my people, to my law: incline your ears to the words of my mouth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Aperiam in parabolis os meum ;  loquar propositiones ab initio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֶפְתְּחָ֣ה בְמָשָׁ֣ל פִּ֑י אַבִּ֥יעָה חִ֝יד֗וֹת מִנִּי־קֶֽדֶם׃
verses.king_james: I will open my mouth in a parable: I will utter dark sayings of old:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quanta audivimus, et cognovimus ea,  et patres nostri narraverunt nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֣ר שָׁ֭מַעְנוּ וַנֵּדָעֵ֑ם וַ֝אֲבוֹתֵ֗ינוּ סִפְּרוּ־לָֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Which we have heard and known, and our fathers have told us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non sunt occultata a filiis eorum in generatione altera,  narrantes laudes Domini et virtutes ejus,  et mirabilia ejus quæ fecit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֤א נְכַחֵ֨ד ׀ מִבְּנֵיהֶ֗ם לְד֥וֹר אַחֲר֗וֹן מְֽ֭סַפְּרִים תְּהִלּ֣וֹת יְהֹוָ֑ה וֶעֱזוּז֥וֹ וְ֝נִפְלְאֹתָ֗יו אֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָֽׂה׃
verses.king_james: We will not hide them from their children, shewing to the generation to come the praises of the Lord, and his strength, and his wonderful works that he hath done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et suscitavit testimonium in Jacob,  et legem posuit in Israël,  quanta mandavit patribus nostris  nota facere ea filiis suis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּ֤קֶם עֵד֨וּת ׀ בְּֽיַעֲקֹ֗ב וְתוֹרָה֮ שָׂ֤ם בְּיִשְׂרָ֫אֵ֥ל אֲשֶׁ֣ר צִ֭וָּה אֶת־אֲבוֹתֵ֑ינוּ לְ֝הוֹדִיעָ֗ם לִבְנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: For he established a testimony in Jacob, and appointed a law in Israel, which he commanded our fathers, that they should make them known to their children:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut cognoscat generatio altera :  filii qui nascentur et exsurgent,  et narrabunt filiis suis,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְמַ֤עַן יֵדְע֨וּ ׀ דּ֣וֹר אַ֭חֲרוֹן בָּנִ֣ים יִוָּלֵ֑דוּ יָ֝קֻ֗מוּ וִיסַפְּר֥וּ לִבְנֵיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: That the generation to come might know them, even the children which should be born; who should arise and declare them to their children:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut ponant in Deo spem suam,  et non obliviscantur operum Dei,  et mandata ejus exquirant :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיָשִׂ֥ימוּ בֵאלֹהִ֗ים כִּ֫סְלָ֥ם וְלֹ֣א יִ֭שְׁכְּחוּ מַ֥עַלְלֵי־אֵ֑ל וּמִצְוֺתָ֥יו יִנְצֹֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: That they might set their hope in God, and not forget the works of God, but keep his commandments:

verses.latin_vulgate: ne fiant, sicut patres eorum,  generatio prava et exasperans ;  generatio quæ non direxit cor suum,  et non est creditus cum Deo spiritus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְלֹ֤א יִהְי֨וּ ׀ כַּאֲבוֹתָ֗ם דּוֹר֮ סוֹרֵ֢ר וּמֹ֫רֶ֥ה דּ֭וֹר לֹא־הֵכִ֣ין לִבּ֑וֹ וְלֹֽא־נֶאֶמְנָ֖ה אֶת־אֵ֣ל רוּחֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And might not be as their fathers, a stubborn and rebellious generation; a generation that set not their heart aright, and whose spirit was not stedfast with God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Filii Ephrem, intendentes et mittentes arcum,  conversi sunt in die belli.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּֽנֵי־אֶפְרַ֗יִם נוֹשְׁקֵ֥י רֽוֹמֵי־קָ֑שֶׁת הָ֝פְכ֗וּ בְּי֣וֹם קְרָֽב׃
verses.king_james: The children of Ephraim, being armed, and carrying bows, turned back in the day of battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non custodierunt testamentum Dei,  et in lege ejus noluerunt ambulare.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֣א שָׁ֭מְרוּ בְּרִ֣ית אֱלֹהִ֑ים וּ֝בְתוֹרָת֗וֹ מֵאֲנ֥וּ לָלֶֽכֶת׃
verses.king_james: They kept not the covenant of God, and refused to walk in his law;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et obliti sunt benefactorum ejus,  et mirabilium ejus quæ ostendit eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכְּח֥וּ עֲלִילוֹתָ֑יו וְ֝נִפְלְאוֹתָ֗יו אֲשֶׁ֣ר הֶרְאָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And forgat his works, and his wonders that he had shewed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Coram patribus eorum fecit mirabilia  in terra Ægypti, in campo Taneos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֶ֣גֶד אֲ֭בוֹתָם עָ֣שָׂה פֶ֑לֶא בְּאֶ֖רֶץ מִצְרַ֣יִם שְׂדֵה־צֹֽעַן׃
verses.king_james: Marvellous things did he in the sight of their fathers, in the land of Egypt, in the field of Zoan.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interrupit mare, et perduxit eos,  et statuit aquas quasi in utre :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּ֣קַע יָ֭ם וַיַּעֲבִירֵ֑ם וַֽיַּצֶּב־מַ֥יִם כְּמוֹ־נֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: He divided the sea, and caused them to pass through; and he made the waters to stand as an heap.

verses.latin_vulgate: et deduxit eos in nube diei,  et tota nocte in illuminatione ignis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּנְחֵ֣ם בֶּעָנָ֣ן יוֹמָ֑ם וְכׇל־הַ֝לַּ֗יְלָה בְּא֣וֹר אֵֽשׁ׃
verses.king_james: In the daytime also he led them with a cloud, and all the night with a light of fire.

verses.latin_vulgate: Interrupit petram in eremo,  et adaquavit eos velut in abysso multa.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְבַקַּ֣ע צֻ֭רִים בַּמִּדְבָּ֑ר וַ֝יַּ֗שְׁקְ כִּתְהֹמ֥וֹת רַבָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: He clave the rocks in the wilderness, and gave them drink as out of the great depths.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et eduxit aquam de petra,  et deduxit tamquam flumina aquas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיּוֹצִ֣א נוֹזְלִ֣ים מִסָּ֑לַע וַיּ֖וֹרֶד כַּנְּהָר֣וֹת מָֽיִם׃
verses.king_james: He brought streams also out of the rock, and caused waters to run down like rivers.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et apposuerunt adhuc peccare ei ;  in iram excitaverunt Excelsum in inaquoso.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיּוֹסִ֣יפוּ ע֭וֹד לַחֲטֹא־ל֑וֹ לַֽמְר֥וֹת עֶ֝לְי֗וֹן בַּצִּיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: And they sinned yet more against him by provoking the most High in the wilderness.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tentaverunt Deum in cordibus suis,  ut peterent escas animabus suis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְנַסּוּ־אֵ֥ל בִּלְבָבָ֑ם לִֽשְׁאׇל־אֹ֥כֶל לְנַפְשָֽׁם׃
verses.king_james: And they tempted God in their heart by asking meat for their lust.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et male locuti sunt de Deo ;  dixerunt : Numquid poterit Deus parare mensam in deserto ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְדַבְּר֗וּ בֵּאלֹ֫הִ֥ים אָ֭מְרוּ הֲי֣וּכַל אֵ֑ל לַעֲרֹ֥ךְ שֻׁ֝לְחָ֗ן בַּמִּדְבָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: Yea, they spake against God; they said, Can God furnish a table in the wilderness?

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam percussit petram, et fluxerunt aquæ,  et torrentes inundaverunt.  Numquid et panem poterit dare,  aut parare mensam populo suo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵ֤ן הִכָּה־צ֨וּר ׀ וַיָּז֣וּבוּ מַיִם֮ וּנְחָלִ֢ים יִ֫שְׁטֹ֥פוּ הֲגַם־לֶ֭חֶם י֣וּכַל תֵּ֑ת אִם־יָכִ֖ין שְׁאֵ֣ר לְעַמּֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Behold, he smote the rock, that the waters gushed out, and the streams overflowed; can he give bread also? can he provide flesh for his people?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo audivit Dominus et distulit ;  et ignis accensus est in Jacob,  et ira ascendit in Israël :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֤ן ׀ שָׁמַ֥ע יְהֹוָ֗ה וַֽיִּתְעַ֫בָּ֥ר וְ֭אֵשׁ נִשְּׂקָ֣ה בְיַעֲקֹ֑ב וְגַם־אַ֝֗ף עָלָ֥ה בְיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: Therefore the Lord heard this, and was wroth: so a fire was kindled against Jacob, and anger also came up against Israel;

verses.latin_vulgate: quia non crediderunt in Deo,  nec speraverunt in salutari ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א הֶ֭אֱמִינוּ בֵּאלֹהִ֑ים וְלֹ֥א בָ֝טְח֗וּ בִּישׁוּעָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Because they believed not in God, and trusted not in his salvation:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et mandavit nubibus desuper,  et januas cæli aperuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְצַ֣ו שְׁחָקִ֣ים מִמָּ֑עַל וְדַלְתֵ֖י שָׁמַ֣יִם פָּתָֽח׃
verses.king_james: Though he had commanded the clouds from above, and opened the doors of heaven,

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pluit illis manna ad manducandum,  et panem cæli dedit eis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּמְטֵ֬ר עֲלֵיהֶ֣ם מָ֣ן לֶאֱכֹ֑ל וּדְגַן־שָׁ֝מַ֗יִם נָ֣תַן לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And had rained down manna upon them to eat, and had given them of the corn of heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: Panem angelorum manducavit homo ;  cibaria misit eis in abundantia.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֶ֣חֶם אַ֭בִּירִים אָ֣כַל אִ֑ישׁ צֵידָ֬ה שָׁלַ֖ח לָהֶ֣ם לָשֹֽׂבַע׃
verses.king_james: Man did eat angels’ food: he sent them meat to the full.

verses.latin_vulgate: Transtulit austrum de cælo,  et induxit in virtute sua africum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַסַּ֣ע קָ֭דִים בַּשָּׁמָ֑יִם וַיְנַהֵ֖ג בְּעֻזּ֣וֹ תֵימָֽן׃
verses.king_james: He caused an east wind to blow in the heaven: and by his power he brought in the south wind.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pluit super eos sicut pulverem carnes,  et sicut arenam maris volatilia pennata.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּמְטֵ֬ר עֲלֵיהֶ֣ם כֶּעָפָ֣ר שְׁאֵ֑ר וּֽכְח֥וֹל יַ֝מִּ֗ים ע֣וֹף כָּנָֽף׃
verses.king_james: He rained flesh also upon them as dust, and feathered fowls like as the sand of the sea:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ceciderunt in medio castrorum eorum,  circa tabernacula eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יַּפֵּל בְּקֶ֣רֶב מַחֲנֵ֑הוּ סָ֝בִ֗יב לְמִשְׁכְּנֹתָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: And he let it fall in the midst of their camp, round about their habitations.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et manducaverunt, et saturati sunt nimis,  et desiderium eorum attulit eis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּאכְל֣וּ וַיִּשְׂבְּע֣וּ מְאֹ֑ד וְ֝תַאֲוָתָ֗ם יָבִ֥א לָהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: So they did eat, and were well filled: for he gave them their own desire;

verses.latin_vulgate: non sunt fraudati a desiderio suo.  Adhuc escæ eorum erant in ore ipsorum,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־זָ֥רוּ מִתַּאֲוָתָ֑ם ע֝֗וֹד אׇכְלָ֥ם בְּפִיהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: They were not estranged from their lust. But while their meat was yet in their mouths,

verses.latin_vulgate: et ira Dei ascendit super eos :  et occidit pingues eorum,  et electos Israël impedivit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַ֤ף אֱלֹהִ֨ים ׀ עָ֘לָ֤ה בָהֶ֗ם וַֽ֭יַּהֲרֹג בְּמִשְׁמַנֵּיהֶ֑ם וּבַחוּרֵ֖י יִשְׂרָאֵ֣ל הִכְרִֽיעַ׃
verses.king_james: The wrath of God came upon them, and slew the fattest of them, and smote down the chosen men of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: In omnibus his peccaverunt adhuc,  et non crediderunt in mirabilibus ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּכׇל־זֹ֥את חָֽטְאוּ־ע֑וֹד וְלֹֽא־הֶ֝אֱמִ֗ינוּ בְּנִפְלְאוֹתָֽיו׃
verses.king_james: For all this they sinned still, and believed not for his wondrous works.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et defecerunt in vanitate dies eorum,  et anni eorum cum festinatione.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְכַל־בַּהֶ֥בֶל יְמֵיהֶ֑ם וּ֝שְׁנוֹתָ֗ם בַּבֶּהָלָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Therefore their days did he consume in vanity, and their years in trouble.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum occideret eos, quærebant eum et revertebantur,  et diluculo veniebant ad eum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־הֲרָגָ֥ם וּדְרָשׁ֑וּהוּ וְ֝שָׁ֗בוּ וְשִֽׁחֲרוּ־אֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: When he slew them, then they sought him: and they returned and enquired early after God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et rememorati sunt quia Deus adjutor est eorum,  et Deus excelsus redemptor eorum est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽ֭יִּזְכְּרוּ כִּֽי־אֱלֹהִ֣ים צוּרָ֑ם וְאֵ֥ל עֶ֝לְי֗וֹן גֹּאֲלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And they remembered that God was their rock, and the high God their redeemer.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dilexerunt eum in ore suo,  et lingua sua mentiti sunt ei ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְפַתּ֥וּהוּ בְּפִיהֶ֑ם וּ֝בִלְשׁוֹנָ֗ם יְכַזְּבוּ־לֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: Nevertheless they did flatter him with their mouth, and they lied unto him with their tongues.

verses.latin_vulgate: cor autem eorum non erat rectum cum eo,  nec fideles habiti sunt in testamento ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֭לִבָּם לֹא־נָכ֣וֹן עִמּ֑וֹ וְלֹ֥א נֶ֝אֶמְנ֗וּ בִּבְרִיתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: For their heart was not right with him, neither were they stedfast in his covenant.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse autem est misericors,  et propitius fiet peccatis eorum,  et non disperdet eos.  Et abundavit ut averteret iram suam,  et non accendit omnem iram suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְה֤וּא רַח֨וּם ׀ יְכַפֵּ֥ר עָוֺן֮ וְֽלֹא־יַֽ֫שְׁחִ֥ית וְ֭הִרְבָּה לְהָשִׁ֣יב אַפּ֑וֹ וְלֹא־יָ֝עִ֗יר כׇּל־חֲמָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: But he, being full of compassion, forgave their iniquity, and destroyed them not: yea, many a time turned he his anger away, and did not stir up all his wrath.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et recordatus est quia caro sunt,  spiritus vadens et non rediens.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יִּזְכֹּר כִּֽי־בָשָׂ֣ר הֵ֑מָּה ר֥וּחַ ה֝וֹלֵ֗ךְ וְלֹ֣א יָשֽׁוּב׃
verses.king_james: For he remembered that they were but flesh; a wind that passeth away, and cometh not again.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quoties exacerbaverunt eum in deserto ;  in iram concitaverunt eum in inaquoso ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כַּ֭מָּה יַמְר֣וּהוּ בַמִּדְבָּ֑ר יַ֝עֲצִיב֗וּהוּ בִּישִׁימֽוֹן׃
verses.king_james: How oft did they provoke him in the wilderness, and grieve him in the desert!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et conversi sunt, et tentaverunt Deum,  et sanctum Israël exacerbaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּשׁ֣וּבוּ וַיְנַסּ֣וּ אֵ֑ל וּקְד֖וֹשׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֣ל הִתְוֽוּ׃
verses.king_james: Yea, they turned back and tempted God, and limited the Holy One of Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non sunt recordati manus ejus,  die qua redemit eos de manu tribulantis :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹא־זָכְר֥וּ אֶת־יָד֑וֹ י֝֗וֹם אֲֽשֶׁר־פָּדָ֥ם מִנִּי־צָֽר׃
verses.king_james: They remembered not his hand, nor the day when he delivered them from the enemy.

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut posuit in Ægypto signa sua,  et prodigia sua in campo Taneos ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁר־שָׂ֣ם בְּ֭מִצְרַיִם אֹתוֹתָ֑יו וּ֝מוֹפְתָ֗יו בִּשְׂדֵה־צֹֽעַן׃
verses.king_james: How he had wrought his signs in Egypt, and his wonders in the field of Zoan:

verses.latin_vulgate: et convertit in sanguinem flumina eorum,  et imbres eorum, ne biberent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּהֲפֹ֣ךְ לְ֭דָם יְאֹרֵיהֶ֑ם וְ֝נֹזְלֵיהֶ֗ם בַּל־יִשְׁתָּיֽוּן׃
verses.king_james: And had turned their rivers into blood; and their floods, that they could not drink.

verses.latin_vulgate: Misit in eos cœnomyiam, et comedit eos,  et ranam, et disperdidit eos ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְשַׁלַּ֬ח בָּהֶ֣ם עָ֭רֹב וַיֹּאכְלֵ֑ם וּ֝צְפַרְדֵּ֗עַ וַתַּשְׁחִיתֵֽם׃
verses.king_james: He sent divers sorts of flies among them, which devoured them; and frogs, which destroyed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: et dedit ærugini fructus eorum,  et labores eorum locustæ ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֣ן לֶחָסִ֣יל יְבוּלָ֑ם וִ֝יגִיעָ֗ם לָאַרְבֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: He gave also their increase unto the caterpiller, and their labour unto the locust.

verses.latin_vulgate: et occidit in grandine vineas eorum,  et moros eorum in pruina ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַהֲרֹ֣ג בַּבָּרָ֣ד גַּפְנָ֑ם וְ֝שִׁקְמוֹתָ֗ם בַּחֲנָמַֽל׃
verses.king_james: He destroyed their vines with hail, and their sycomore trees with frost.

verses.latin_vulgate: et tradidit grandini jumenta eorum,  et possessionem eorum igni ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּסְגֵּ֣ר לַבָּרָ֣ד בְּעִירָ֑ם וּ֝מִקְנֵיהֶ֗ם לָרְשָׁפִֽים׃
verses.king_james: He gave up their cattle also to the hail, and their flocks to hot thunderbolts.

verses.latin_vulgate: misit in eos iram indignationis suæ,  indignationem, et iram, et tribulationem,  immissiones per angelos malos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְשַׁלַּח־בָּ֨ם ׀ חֲר֬וֹן אַפּ֗וֹ עֶבְרָ֣ה וָזַ֣עַם וְצָרָ֑ה מִ֝שְׁלַ֗חַת מַלְאֲכֵ֥י רָעִֽים׃
verses.king_james: He cast upon them the fierceness of his anger, wrath, and indignation, and trouble, by sending evil angels among them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Viam fecit semitæ iræ suæ :  non pepercit a morte animabus eorum,  et jumenta eorum in morte conclusit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יְפַלֵּ֥ס נָתִ֗יב לְאַ֫פּ֥וֹ לֹא־חָשַׂ֣ךְ מִמָּ֣וֶת נַפְשָׁ֑ם וְ֝חַיָּתָ֗ם לַדֶּ֥בֶר הִסְגִּֽיר׃
verses.king_james: He made a way to his anger; he spared not their soul from death, but gave their life over to the pestilence;

verses.latin_vulgate: et percussit omne primogenitum in terra Ægypti ;  primitias omnis laboris eorum in tabernaculis Cham :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֣ךְ כׇּל־בְּכ֣וֹר בְּמִצְרָ֑יִם רֵאשִׁ֥ית א֝וֹנִ֗ים בְּאׇהֳלֵי־חָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And smote all the firstborn in Egypt; the chief of their strength in the tabernacles of Ham:

verses.latin_vulgate: et abstulit sicut oves populum suum,  et perduxit eos tamquam gregem in deserto :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּסַּ֣ע כַּצֹּ֣אן עַמּ֑וֹ וַֽיְנַהֲגֵ֥ם כַּ֝עֵ֗דֶר בַּמִּדְבָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: But made his own people to go forth like sheep, and guided them in the wilderness like a flock.

verses.latin_vulgate: et deduxit eos in spe, et non timuerunt,  et inimicos eorum operuit mare.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּנְחֵ֣ם לָ֭בֶטַח וְלֹ֣א פָחָ֑דוּ וְאֶת־א֝וֹיְבֵיהֶ֗ם כִּסָּ֥ה הַיָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: And he led them on safely, so that they feared not: but the sea overwhelmed their enemies.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et induxit eos in montem sanctificationis suæ,  montem quem acquisivit dextera ejus ;  et ejecit a facie eorum gentes,  et sorte divisit eis terram in funiculo distributionis ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יְבִיאֵם אֶל־גְּב֣וּל קׇדְשׁ֑וֹ הַר־זֶ֝֗ה קָנְתָ֥ה יְמִינֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And he brought them to the border of his sanctuary, even to this mountain, which his right hand had purchased.

verses.latin_vulgate: et habitare fecit in tabernaculis eorum tribus Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְגָ֤רֶשׁ מִפְּנֵיהֶ֨ם ׀ גּוֹיִ֗ם וַֽ֭יַּפִּילֵם בְּחֶ֣בֶל נַחֲלָ֑ה וַיַּשְׁכֵּ֥ן בְּ֝אׇהֳלֵיהֶ֗ם שִׁבְטֵ֥י יִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: He cast out the heathen also before them, and divided them an inheritance by line, and made the tribes of Israel to dwell in their tents.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tentaverunt, et exacerbaverunt Deum excelsum,  et testimonia ejus non custodierunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְנַסּ֣וּ וַ֭יַּמְרוּ אֶת־אֱלֹהִ֣ים עֶלְי֑וֹן וְ֝עֵדוֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א שָׁמָֽרוּ׃
verses.king_james: Yet they tempted and provoked the most high God, and kept not his testimonies:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et averterunt se, et non servaverunt pactum :  quemadmodum patres eorum, conversi sunt in arcum pravum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּסֹּ֣גוּ וַֽ֭יִּבְגְּדוּ כַּאֲבוֹתָ֑ם נֶ֝הְפְּכ֗וּ כְּקֶ֣שֶׁת רְמִיָּֽה׃
verses.king_james: But turned back, and dealt unfaithfully like their fathers: they were turned aside like a deceitful bow.

verses.latin_vulgate: In iram concitaverunt eum in collibus suis,  et in sculptilibus suis ad æmulationem eum provocaverunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּכְעִיס֥וּהוּ בְּבָמוֹתָ֑ם וּ֝בִפְסִילֵיהֶ֗ם יַקְנִיאֽוּהוּ׃
verses.king_james: For they provoked him to anger with their high places, and moved him to jealousy with their graven images.

verses.latin_vulgate: Audivit Deus, et sprevit,  et ad nihilum redegit valde Israël.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁמַ֣ע אֱ֭לֹהִים וַֽיִּתְעַבָּ֑ר וַיִּמְאַ֥ס מְ֝אֹ֗ד בְּיִשְׂרָאֵֽל׃
verses.king_james: When God heard this, he was wroth, and greatly abhorred Israel:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et repulit tabernaculum Silo,  tabernaculum suum, ubi habitavit in hominibus.
verses.king_james: So that he forsook the tabernacle of Shiloh, the tent which he placed among men;

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tradidit in captivitatem virtutem eorum,  et pulchritudinem eorum in manus inimici.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּתֵּ֣ן לַשְּׁבִ֣י עֻזּ֑וֹ וְֽתִפְאַרְתּ֥וֹ בְיַד־צָֽר׃
verses.king_james: And delivered his strength into captivity, and his glory into the enemy’s hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et conclusit in gladio populum suum,  et hæreditatem suam sprevit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּסְגֵּ֣ר לַחֶ֣רֶב עַמּ֑וֹ וּ֝בְנַחֲלָת֗וֹ הִתְעַבָּֽר׃
verses.king_james: He gave his people over also unto the sword; and was wroth with his inheritance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Juvenes eorum comedit ignis,  et virgines eorum non sunt lamentatæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּחוּרָ֥יו אָכְלָה־אֵ֑שׁ וּ֝בְתוּלֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א הוּלָּֽלוּ׃
verses.king_james: The fire consumed their young men; and their maidens were not given to marriage.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sacerdotes eorum in gladio ceciderunt,  et viduæ eorum non plorabantur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֭הֲנָיו בַּחֶ֣רֶב נָפָ֑לוּ וְ֝אַלְמְנֹתָ֗יו לֹ֣א תִבְכֶּֽינָה׃
verses.king_james: Their priests fell by the sword; and their widows made no lamentation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et excitatus est tamquam dormiens Dominus,  tamquam potens crapulatus a vino.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקַ֖ץ כְּיָשֵׁ֥ן ׀ אֲדֹנָ֑י כְּ֝גִבּ֗וֹר מִתְרוֹנֵ֥ן מִיָּֽיִן׃
verses.king_james: Then the Lord awaked as one out of sleep, and like a mighty man that shouteth by reason of wine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et percussit inimicos suos in posteriora ;  opprobrium sempiternum dedit illis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּךְ־צָרָ֥יו אָח֑וֹר חֶרְפַּ֥ת ע֝וֹלָ֗ם נָ֣תַן לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.king_james: And he smote his enemies in the hinder parts: he put them to a perpetual reproach.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et repulit tabernaculum Joseph,  et tribum Ephraim non elegit :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יִּמְאַס בְּאֹ֣הֶל יוֹסֵ֑ף וּֽבְשֵׁ֥בֶט אֶ֝פְרַ֗יִם לֹ֣א בָחָֽר׃
verses.king_james: Moreover he refused the tabernacle of Joseph, and chose not the tribe of Ephraim:

verses.latin_vulgate: sed elegit tribum Juda,  montem Sion, quem dilexit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יִּבְחַר אֶת־שֵׁ֣בֶט יְהוּדָ֑ה אֶֽת־הַ֥ר צִ֝יּ֗וֹן אֲשֶׁ֣ר אָהֵֽב׃
verses.king_james: But chose the tribe of Judah, the mount Zion which he loved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ædificavit sicut unicornium sanctificium suum,  in terra quam fundavit in sæcula.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּ֣בֶן כְּמֽוֹ־רָ֭מִים מִקְדָּשׁ֑וֹ כְּ֝אֶ֗רֶץ יְסָדָ֥הּ לְעוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: And he built his sanctuary like high palaces, like the earth which he hath established for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et elegit David, servum suum,  et sustulit eum de gregibus ovium ;  de post fœtantes accepit eum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יִּבְחַר בְּדָוִ֣ד עַבְדּ֑וֹ וַ֝יִּקָּחֵ֗הוּ מִֽמִּכְלְאֹ֥ת צֹֽאן׃
verses.king_james: He chose David also his servant, and took him from the sheepfolds:

verses.latin_vulgate: pascere Jacob servum suum,  et Israël hæreditatem suam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מֵאַחַ֥ר עָל֗וֹת הֱבִ֫יא֥וֹ לִ֭רְעוֹת בְּיַעֲקֹ֣ב עַמּ֑וֹ וּ֝בְיִשְׂרָאֵ֗ל נַחֲלָתֽוֹ׃
verses.king_james: From following the ewes great with young he brought him to feed Jacob his people, and Israel his inheritance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pavit eos in innocentia cordis sui,  et in intellectibus manuum suarum deduxit eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַ֭יִּרְעֵם כְּתֹ֣ם לְבָב֑וֹ וּבִתְבוּנ֖וֹת כַּפָּ֣יו יַנְחֵֽם׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: So he fed them according to the integrity of his heart; and guided them by the skilfulness of his hands.
